questions.question: Which of the following is the defining characteristic of an embedded system?

options.a: It can run any general-purpose desktop operating system
options.b: It is programmed exclusively in high-level Python script at runtime
options.c: **It is a combination of hardware and software designed for a specific, dedicated task ✓**
options.d: It lacks any form of microprocessors or control logic

questions.question: What is the primary economic driver for resource sharing in enterprise networks?

options.b: **To reduce hardware duplication and lower capital expenditure ✓**
options.c: To eliminate the need for firewalls
options.d: To bypass local network access levels

questions.question: Why is the software in an embedded system referred to as 'firmware'?

options.a: It is physically rigid and cannot be upgraded
options.b: **It is embedded directly into non-volatile hardware memory like ROM or Flash ✓**
options.c: It only runs when the hardware is kept at cold temperatures
options.d: It is supplied by the computer chassis manufacturer

questions.question: Which processor type is hardwired to execute a single specific task and cannot be reprogrammed?
In a Microprocessor, which components are external to the main CPU chip?

options.c: The CPU registers and the Instruction Decoder
options.d: The Program Counter and Control Unit

questions.question: Which architecture utilizes a single shared memory and bus for both instructions and data?
What is the 'Von Neumann Bottleneck'?

options.a: The CPU running out of register space during a multiply-accumulate instruction
options.b: The inability of the CPU to compile assembly code directly
options.c: **The CPU cannot read data and fetch instructions at the same time because they share the same bus ✓**
options.d: Data corrupting when travelling through a fiber optic cable

questions.question: Which architecture is preferred for microcontrollers due to its ability to fetch instructions and read data in parallel?
A System-on-Chip (SoC) is best described as:

options.a: A microprocessor connected to external RAM on a motherboard
options.b: **An integrated circuit that incorporates a CPU, memory, I/O ports, and radio modules on a single silicon die ✓**
options.c: A cloud server cluster running virtual machine controllers
options.d: A physical star topology network using passive hubs

questions.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic of General Purpose Processors (GPPs)?

options.a: Highly optimized for low-power battery operations
options.b: Strictly limited to single-function industrial applications
options.c: **High computational power with general instruction sets, but high cost and power draw ✓**
options.d: Must be stored in a write-once read-many ROM chip

questions.question: Which of the following is an application of an Application Specific Instruction Set Processor (ASIP)?

options.a: Running standard office productivity suites on a desktop
options.b: **Digital signal processing and video encoding inside a camera ✓**
options.c: Hosting public websites on a virtual private server
options.d: Allocating IP addresses on a DHCP server

questions.question: Which design metric is typically a major constraint in battery-operated consumer IoT devices?

options.c: **Low power consumption and small physical footprint ✓**

questions.question: What does 'autonomous' mean in the definition of a computer network?

options.a: The network runs entirely without human administrators
options.b: **Each connected computer is capable of running independently and possesses its own CPU ✓**
options.c: The cabling must route itself automatically using smart protocols
options.d: All devices must be assigned the same IP prefix

questions.question: The CPU instruction cycle consists of which sequential phases?
Which CPU register holds the memory address of the next instruction to be fetched?
In which architecture can program instructions and data have different word widths (e.g. 14-bit instructions and 8-bit data)?
Which of the following is a classic example of a Microcontroller?
What type of memory is typically used to store the bootloader and firmware in a microcontroller?
What is the primary function of a Digital Signal Processor (DSP)?

options.a: To route IP packets across WAN boundaries
options.b: **To perform rapid mathematical operations like multiply-accumulate on digitized signals ✓**
options.c: To convert analog phone lines to digital Ethernet ports
options.d: To act as a central controller in Star topologies

questions.question: Which design aspect is a disadvantage of Harvard Architecture compared to Von Neumann?

options.a: Slower execution speeds due to shared buses
options.c: **Increased hardware complexity and cost due to dual bus routing ✓**

questions.question: What is the relationship between power supply voltage and gate switching speed in digital logic circuits?

options.b: **Higher voltage increases switching speed, reducing propagation delay ✓**
options.c: Voltage does not affect switching speed
options.d: Switching speed is determined only by the crystal length

questions.question: Which logic level voltage range is commonly used for modern low-power mobile SoCs?
What is the purpose of a Quartz Crystal in an oscillator circuit?

options.a: To act as a voltage filter
options.b: **To generate a highly stable, precise clock frequency using piezoelectric properties ✓**
options.c: To detect temperature variations in the silicon die
options.d: To protect the CPU against power surges

questions.question: What type of reset circuit uses a capacitor and a resistor to hold the reset pin low for a short duration after power-on?
A Watchdog Timer (WDT) is primarily designed to:

options.a: Count external input pulses from sensors
options.b: **Recover the microcontroller from software hangs by triggering a reset if not cleared regularly ✓**
options.c: Measure the execution speed of the main loop
options.d: Perform encryption calculations on data packets

questions.question: Brown-out Detection (BOD) circuits monitor which parameter?

options.b: **Power supply voltage levels, resetting the CPU if voltage drops below a threshold ✓**
options.c: The amount of ambient light surrounding the device
options.d: Unicast packet collisions on Ethernet ports

questions.question: Which type of memory is volatile and loses its data when power is disconnected?
What is the difference between EEPROM and Flash memory?

options.a: EEPROM is volatile while Flash is non-volatile
options.b: **EEPROM allows byte-level erasing, while Flash is erased in large blocks ✓**
options.c: Flash memory requires constant refreshing while EEPROM does not
options.d: EEPROM is much faster than Flash memory

questions.question: Which of the following is classified as an active sensor?

options.a: **LIDAR (requires sending a light pulse to measure reflection) ✓**

questions.question: An LDR (Light Dependent Resistor) exhibits which electrical behavior when light intensity increases?

options.c: It generates an electrical current directly

questions.question: How does an LM35 temperature sensor represent temperature values?

options.a: By outputting a 10-bit SPI digital word
options.b: **By outputting a linear analog voltage (10mV per degree Celsius) ✓**
options.c: By altering its capacitance by 1nF per degree

questions.question: Which sensor would be most appropriate to measure pressure changes in a pneumatic system?
An Actuator is best defined as a device that:

options.a: Converts physical quantities into electrical signals
options.b: **Converts electrical energy or control signals into physical action/motion ✓**

questions.question: Which motor allows precise control of angular position, velocity, and acceleration without feedback sensors?
How does a Solenoid valve convert electrical energy into mechanical movement?

options.a: By using a piezoelectric crystal array
options.b: **By passing current through a coil to generate an electromagnetic field that pulls a plunger ✓**
options.c: By using a gear train and brushes

questions.question: A servo motor differs from a standard DC motor because:

options.b: **It includes a feedback sensor (potentiometer) and control circuit to maintain a precise angle ✓**
options.c: It runs directly on AC power
options.d: It cannot be controlled by a microcontroller

questions.question: Which type of reset occurs when the input supply voltage drops below the safe limit for CPU operations?
What is the advantage of using a crystal oscillator over an RC oscillator?

options.a: Crystal oscillators are cheaper to integrate on a silicon die
options.b: **Crystal oscillators provide a much higher frequency stability and precision ✓**
options.c: RC oscillators require external quartz inputs
options.d: Crystal oscillators do not require power supply lines

questions.question: Which component acts as an electrical barrier or switch to control high-voltage/high-current devices using low-voltage microcontroller pins?
What will happen if software fails to 'kick' or clear the Watchdog Timer before its counter overflows?

options.a: The CPU halts instruction execution indefinitely
options.b: **The Watchdog circuit triggers a hardware reset of the microcontroller ✓**
options.c: The CPU automatically enters sleep mode
options.d: The system switches to secondary flash memory

questions.question: Which of the following represents the basic physical design of an IoT system?

options.c: A centralized mainframe with dumb terminals

questions.question: In the logical design of an IoT system, what is the role of the 'Communication' functional block?

options.b: **Handling the communication protocols and data exchange between devices and servers ✓**
options.d: Running machine learning algorithms on the device

questions.question: Which IoT communication model is characterized by the client sending a request and the server responding immediately, in a synchronous, stateless manner?
In a Publish-Subscribe communication model, which entity is responsible for routing messages from publishers to subscribers based on topics?
Which IoT communication model uses a persistent, bidirectional, full-duplex connection over a single TCP socket?
What is a key difference between M2M (Machine-to-Machine) and IoT (Internet of Things)?

options.a: M2M utilizes cloud databases, whereas IoT relies on direct point-to-point links
options.b: **M2M typically uses isolated point-to-point links; IoT uses global internet networks and cloud architectures ✓**
options.c: M2M is highly scalable compared to IoT
options.d: IoT does not support wireless communication

questions.question: An IoT system is designed with a single device that collects data, stores it in a local database, and displays it via a local web interface without any cloud dependency. This matches which IoT Level?
In IoT Level-3, what is the primary role of the Cloud?

options.a: The cloud only performs local sensor reading
options.b: **Data is stored and analyzed in the cloud, while the local device only senses and sends data ✓**
options.d: The cloud manages the local mesh topology

questions.question: Which IoT level is characterized by multiple independent nodes, a centralized cloud database, and observer nodes running analytical applications?
In the Publish-Subscribe model, publishers and subscribers do not need to know about each other. This is known as:
Which functional block of an IoT system handles device authentication, authorization, and data encryption?
What is the primary function of the 'Device' block in the physical design of IoT?

options.b: **Sensing, actuation, data processing, and local control functions ✓**

questions.question: Which IoT level is best suited for complex, wide-area applications with high volumes of data, requiring multiple end-nodes and cloud-based big data analytics?
The Push-Pull communication model is best described as:

options.a: A synchronous model where the client pulls data from the server
options.b: **An asynchronous model where queues decouple publishers and consumers, pushing data from producer to queue and pulling from queue to consumer ✓**

questions.question: In which M2M component is the communication logic typically embedded?
Which protocol is commonly used to establish the Exclusive Pair communication model over the web?
What type of communication model does MQTT utilize?
In IoT logical design, which block provides services like device discovery, device registry, and status monitoring?
Which of the following is a primary domain application for IoT?

options.b: **Smart Cities, Smart Grid, and Industrial Automation ✓**

questions.question: M2M systems generally emphasize which aspect of network communication?
Which microcontroller chip serves as the main processor on a standard Arduino Uno board?
How many digital input/output pins are available on the Arduino Uno board?
How many pins on the Arduino Uno support Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) output?
How many analog input pins are available on the Arduino Uno board?
The built-in Analog-to-Digital Converter (ADC) on the Arduino Uno has what resolution?
What are the two mandatory functions in every Arduino sketch?
What is the function of the `setup()` block in Arduino programming?

options.a: It runs continuously to process calculations
options.b: **It runs once at startup to initialize pin modes, serial communication, and variables ✓**
options.c: It compiles the binary code to flash memory
options.d: It monitors the watchdog timer reset state

questions.question: Which function is used to configure a specific pin as an INPUT or OUTPUT?
Which function is used to output a HIGH or LOW digital voltage state to a pin?
What will `analogRead(A0)` return when the voltage on pin A0 is exactly 2.5V (assuming a 5.0V reference)?
Which function is used to generate a Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) signal on a PWM-enabled pin?
What is the range of values that can be passed to the `analogWrite()` function?
To establish serial communication between the Arduino Uno and a PC at 9600 baud, what command is used in `setup()`?
How is the built-in LED on the Arduino Uno board mapped to digital pins?
When connecting an LED to a digital output pin on the Arduino, why is a resistor placed in series with the LED?

options.a: To increase the voltage level to the LED
options.b: **To limit the current and prevent burning out the LED and microcontroller pin ✓**
options.d: To act as a pull-up resistor

questions.question: What function is used to create a non-blocking delay or check elapsed time in milliseconds since the Arduino board began running?
What is the problem with using the `delay(1000)` function in an interactive sensor control system?

options.a: It draws high power from the supply
options.b: **It blocks instruction execution, preventing the microcontroller from reading sensor inputs during the delay ✓**
options.d: It overwrites the bootloader flash sectors

questions.question: Which of the following lines correctly configures an analog pin A0 as digital input with an internal pull-up resistor?
Which protocol is natively used by the ATmega328P to load programs through the USB port without needing an external hardware programmer?
If you connect an LM35 temperature sensor output to Arduino pin A1, what code formula converts the raw analog reading `val` to temperature in Celsius (assuming 5V reference)?
Which protocol is the standard physical and MAC layer specification for low-power wireless personal area networks (LR-WPANs) like ZigBee?
What is the primary frequency band used globally by IEEE 802.15.4?
Which wireless protocol is designed specifically for short-range low-power consumption in battery-powered devices like beacons and fitness trackers?
What is the main function of the 6LoWPAN protocol layer?

options.a: Providing application interfaces like HTTP requests
options.b: **Header compression and fragmentation to transmit IPv6 packets over low-power IEEE 802.15.4 links ✓**

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model does 6LoWPAN operate between?
MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) is built on top of which transport layer protocol?
CoAP (Constrained Application Protocol) is designed as a lightweight alternative to HTTP. It runs over which transport layer protocol?
Which of the following best contrasts MQTT and CoAP?

options.a: MQTT is connectionless over UDP, while CoAP is connection-oriented over TCP
options.b: **MQTT is many-to-many publish-subscribe over TCP, while CoAP is one-to-one RESTful request-response over UDP ✓**
options.c: MQTT uses more bandwidth than HTTP
options.d: CoAP does not support GET and POST methods

questions.question: In MQTT, what is a 'topic'?

options.a: A physical address of the broker
options.b: **A hierarchical string key (e.g. 'home/livingroom/temp') used to categorize and route messages ✓**

questions.question: What are the three Quality of Service (QoS) levels supported by MQTT?
Which IEEE standard family defines Wi-Fi networking?
What CoAP message type requires an explicit acknowledgment from the receiver?
Which application layer protocol uses the URI scheme 'coap://'?
What is the role of an MQTT Broker?

options.b: **It acts as a central server that receives publications and forwards them to subscribers based on topic filters ✓**
options.c: It translates physical signals to optical pulses

questions.question: Which protocol is a RESTful, request-response, web-transfer protocol built specifically for constrained nodes and networks?
In BLE, how are data services organized and exposed?

options.b: **Using GATT (Generic Attribute Profile) services and characteristics ✓**

questions.question: What is the maximum payload size of an IEEE 802.15.4 frame before fragmentation at the 6LoWPAN layer?
Which MQTT QoS level guarantees that a message is delivered exactly once to the subscriber, using a 4-step handshake?
What is the standard IPv6 packet size that 6LoWPAN must compress and fragment to fit into 802.15.4 frames?
Which protocol offers the highest data rate and range but consumes the most power, making it unsuitable for tiny coin-cell battery sensors?
